title: Earnings Call Transcript: Tilray Q3 2026 Results Show Strong Growth
text: Tilray achieved net revenue of $206.7 million, representing 11% year-over-year increase with gross profit reaching $55 million. The company significantly improved its net loss position to $25.2 million from $793.5 million year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA: $10.7 million (19% improvement). Cash position: $264.8 million. Cannabis segment: 31% of revenue. International cannabis growth: 73% year-over-year. International markets drove exceptional growth, with German operations increasing 43% despite ongoing price compression across global markets. Medical cannabis volumes surged—flower up 100% and oils up 90% year-over-year. Tilray Pharma grew 35% to $83 million, with CC Pharma recognized among European pharmaceutical industry leaders. New partnerships expanded pharmacy reach from 13,000 to 16,000 locations. Beverage segment revenue declined to $42.6 million due to strategic margin improvements. CEO Simon stated completion of Project Four Twenty delivered $33 million in annualized cost savings, though aluminum and input costs pressured margins. Management maintained adjusted EBITDA guidance of $62 million-$72 million for fiscal 2026. Two major acquisitions position Tilray as a global lifestyle consumer products company: BrewDog (approximately £40 million) adding ~$225-250 million annual revenue, and Carlsberg Partnership beginning January 2027. CEO Simon emphasized the company's transformation from $50 million in revenue in 2019 to approaching $1 billion on an annualized basis.
